label: Sisters Return Land to Lac du Flambeau Band

comments.text:
In the US, Catholic sisters return Wisconsin land to Ojibwe tribe in a historic first. The Franciscan Sisters of Perpetual Adoration have transferred a lakefront property to the Lac du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa for the same $30,000 they paid in 1966.
buff.ly/iXOhxHj
#ShareGoodNewsToo

Two years ago, the Franciscan Sisters of Perpetual Adoration approached the Lac Du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa, and the original caretakers of the land, with an unheard of proposition: Would they like a piece of their land back? 19thnews.org/2025/11/cath...

Nearly 90 acres is back under the control of the band, whose 47,000-acre reservation hugs the Lake Superior shoreline near the border of Ontario. It is considered the tribe’s largest land return in recent history, tribal leaders say.

I was glad to join the Bad River Band of Lake Superior Chippewa to unveil a new dual-language highway sign to properly recognize their sovereign territory. Folks, here in Wisconsin, we choose to embrace, not erase, our partnerships with Native Nations.

“Norval Morrisseau is a reserve Indian. He is a trapper and handyman. He has a Grade 3 education. Sometimes he is an artist.” Shot at Sandy Lake, Ontario. From Marcel Carriére’s 1967 NFB documentary ‘The Indian Speaks.’ NFB Archives

I am thankful for the Bad River Band of Lake Superior Chippewa

Two years ago, the Franciscan Sisters of Perpetual Adoration, a Catholic congregation, had approached the Lac Du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa, a part of the Ojibwe Nation, and the original caretakers of the land, with an unheard of proposition: Would they like a piece of their land back?

Tribal members in Minnesota and Wisconsin are working to revive the game of Baaga’adowewin, the Ojibwe version of lacrosse that once was a vital community-building tradition around the Great Lakes — but then virtually disappeared during the boarding school era.

Drawing of Sioux men from Eastern Dakota in a canoe, fishing southwestern Lake Superior. By Louis Nicolas, Fishing by the Passinassiouek (La pesche des Sauvages), 1700. Using hand held nets and harpoons to catch fish in river and lakes. #coastalhistory 1/2

great news, i'm here to talk about William Henry Harrison, a fighter in the Indian Wars, who got elected on his fame for defeating Tecumseh's Indian confederacy around the Great Lakes and forcing "treaties" that effectively ceded Ohio and Indiana to white settlement

ICYMI📰The Lac Courte Oreilles Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians are the 10th tribe in Wisconsin to have dual language highway signs added to Wisconsin’s highway system as part of an effort to bring awareness to the Native presence in our state. Read more here:

In a landmark act of reparations, the Franciscan Sisters of Perpetual Adoration returned land in northern Wisconsin to the Lac du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians. The Maywood property, once home.... #chippewa

Ettenbeuren, Bavaria (now Germany), 175 years go today 11 Franciscan tertiaries migrated to Milwaukee and settled on the south of the bay of Lake Michigan. The women founded the Sisters of St. Francis of Assisi. They continue their work to this very day: lakeosfs.org

On the shores of Trout Lake, a Catholic sister and Tribal president sat and made history. The Franciscan Sisters of Perpetual Adoration, approached the Lac Du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa, part of the Ojibwe Nation, and asked if they wanted some land back.

Here in "Milwaukee, Wisconsin," Menominee, Potawatomi, Ojibwe, Oneida, and Ho-Chunk are still represented in tribes and Rez land here. Being where we are on Lake Michigan made Milwaukee a natural gathering place and trade hub, so this land has had a shared stewardship for generations.

The FSPA have transferred its Marywood Franciscan Spirituality Center to the Lac du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa. At NABS, we honor and support these efforts as vital steps in acknowledging the truth and repairing the generational harm caused by the Indian boarding school era.

My Wifes Band is the Chippewas of Nawash, the Great Lakes region has been their home for countless Generations, its always good when Land is returned as the Chippewas of Saugeen reclaimed their beach last year despite the local town spending millions to fight it in court #SaugeenBeach

Lac du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians say it will keep road access open for now on tribal land. The announcement comes in a dispute with a nearby town over easements to non-Native homeowners on tribal land.

I see no evidence of your comments. The “Power Soiux” I found online is a hockey team. The Soiux Indians did sign a treaty with Americans and lived around modern day Lake Superior, norther WI & MN. Wisconsin has sued the Lac Du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians after the tribe restricted walleye and musky harvest to its citizens to protect declining fish populations, prompting a federal court to temporarily block the rule.

sistersreport
A congregation of sisters in Wisconsin made history Oct. 31. The Franciscan Sisters of Perpetual Adoration have sold their spirituality center to the Lac du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians.

Start the day with good news
The Lac Du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa regain rightful control of two acres of their land.
19thnews.org/2025/11/cath...

---
The Lac Courte Oreilles Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians, located in northern Wisconsin, faces a unique set of challenges and opportunities in their pursuit of tribal sovereignty.

Wisconsin Tribal Forestry Initiative
The Target: The Lac Courte Oreilles Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians, located in northern Wisconsin.

## Opportunity
The Lac Courte Oreilles Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians, located in northern Wisconsin, controls over 70,000 acres of forestland. These resources offer a path to economic growth and stronger self‑determination.

Wisconsin Tribal Forestry Initiative The Lac Courte Oreilles Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians can turn 70,000 acres into a revenue engine. A tribally chartered nonprofit will run sustainable forestry and keep sovereignty intact.

It just so happens that my Chippewa tribe's reservation is in Baraga, Michigan in Copper Harbor on Lake Superior. So in the late 1980s all members of our tribe were finally compensated for the taking of the Upper Peninsula for the state of Michigan.

Wisconsin sues tribe over non-Native fishing ban The state insists it has exclusive authority to regulate fishing on 19 lakes within the outer border of the Lac du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians reservation. www.courthousenews.com/wisconsin-su...
